element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary combat mechanic throughout game; foundational to all encounters
Initial enemy type establishing basic threat pattern
Environmental combat objectives; demonstrates game's focus shift away from vehicle gameplay
Core tension system: distance meter between players and pursuing army; game-over condition if distance reaches zero
Objective-driven combat; defeating stage boss progresses rescue narrative
Late-game complication; adds vulnerability to pursuit mechanic
Unique NES variant introducing environmental hazard variety

typical_tension_triggers: Hostage recapture by Crime Ghost forces
Ambush events (e.g., helicopter shot down at stage 3)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Sustained escalation with pursuit-based time pressure; tension builds through stage progression and distance tracking; relief moments tied to objective completion rather than safe zones

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of hand-to-hand combat focus; moderate enemy density; pursuit mechanic established as low threat (distant army)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Intensified melee combat scenarios; pursuing army gradually closes distance; stage objectives require sustained performance; boss encounters increase in lethality
difficulty_curve.late_game: Hostage escort vulnerability amplifies pursuit pressure; Crime Ghost ambushes complicate objective; final boss confrontation (NES) or extraction under maximum pressure (arcade)
notable_spikes: Stage 3: Initial boss defeat followed immediately by helicopter ambush and hostage recapture
Final stages: Combined pressure of pursuing army, hostage escort requirements, and remaining Crime Ghost forces

notable_level_patterns.description: Each stage functions as linear forward movement under constant time pressure from pursuing enemy army. Distance meter serves as implicit timer; reaching zero triggers stage restart. Creates sustained tension without traditional countdown clock.
Stage progression tied to rescue mission narrative beats: infiltration → securing hostage → securing extraction. Each objective completion triggers story event and enemy response escalation.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage 3 (boss defeat triggers ambush and hostage recapture)

notable_level_patterns.description: Weapon scarcity and vehicle absence force emphasis on hand-to-hand combat across all environments. NES stage 4 (aquatic) introduces environmental variety without changing core melee mechanic.
